name: Pip identifies his name
source_quote: So, I called myself Pip, and came to be called Pip.

name: Pip reflects on his family name
source_quote: My father’s family name being Pirrip, and my Christian name Philip...

name: Pip attempts to pronounce his names
source_quote: my infant tongue could make of both names nothing longer or more explicit than Pip.

name: Pip identifies his family name
source_quote: I give Pirrip as my father’s family name

name: Pip references his father's tombstone
source_quote: on the authority of his tombstone

name: Pip mentions his sister
source_quote: my sister,—Mrs. Joe Gargery, who married the blacksmith

name: Pip reflects on never seeing his parents
source_quote: As I never saw my father or my mother

name: Pip notes the absence of photographs
source_quote: for their days were long before the days of photographs

name: Pip derives fancies from tombstones
source_quote: my first fancies regarding what they were like were unreasonably derived from their tombstones

name: Pip imagines his father's appearance
source_quote: The shape of the letters on my father’s, gave me an odd idea that he was a square, stout, dark man, with curly black hair

name: Pip draws a conclusion about his mother
source_quote: I drew a childish conclusion that my mother was freckled and sickly.

name: Pip reflects on the grave markers
source_quote: To five little stone lozenges, each about a foot and a half long, which were arranged in a neat row beside their grave, and were sacred to the memory of five little brothers of mine

name: Pip acknowledges the early death of his brothers
source_quote: who gave up trying to get a living, exceedingly early in that universal struggle

name: Pip expresses a belief about his brothers' existence
source_quote: I am indebted for a belief I religiously entertained that they had all been born on their backs with their hands in their trousers-pockets

name: Pip describes the state of existence of his brothers
source_quote: and had never taken them out in this state of existence

name: Pip perceives the marsh country
source_quote: My first most vivid and broad impression of the identity of things seems to me to have been gained on a memorable raw afternoon towards evening.

name: Pip reflects on the identity of things
source_quote: My first most vivid and broad impression of the identity of things seems to me to have been gained on a memorable raw afternoon towards evening.

name: Pip recognizes the churchyard
source_quote: I found out for certain that this bleak place overgrown with nettles was the churchyard

name: Pip learns about the deceased
source_quote: that Philip Pirrip, late of this parish, and also Georgiana wife of the above, were dead and buried

name: Pip recognizes the marshes
source_quote: the dark flat wilderness beyond the churchyard...was the marshes

name: Pip recognizes the river
source_quote: the low leaden line beyond was the river

name: Pip recognizes the sea
source_quote: the distant savage lair from which the wind was rushing was the sea

name: Pip feels afraid
source_quote: the small bundle of shivers growing afraid of it all

name: Pip begins to cry
source_quote: beginning to cry

name: A man starts up from among the graves
source_quote: as a man started up from among the graves at the side of the church porch.

name: A man shouts to keep quiet
source_quote: “Hold your noise!” cried a terrible voice

name: A man threatens
source_quote: or I’ll cut your throat!

name: A man addresses Pip
source_quote: Keep still, you little devil

name: Fearful man appears
source_quote: A fearful man, all in coarse grey, with a great iron on his leg.

name: Man's appearance described
source_quote: A man with no hat, and with broken shoes, and with an old rag tied round his head.

name: Man's condition detailed
source_quote: A man who had been soaked in water, and smothered in mud, and lamed by stones, and cut by flints, and stung by nettles, and torn by briars.

name: Man's physical state described
source_quote: who limped, and shivered, and glared, and growled; and whose teeth chattered in his head.

name: Man seizes Pip
source_quote: as he seized me by the chin.

name: Pip pleads for his life
source_quote: Oh! Don’t cut my throat, sir,” I pleaded in terror.

name: Pip expresses terror
source_quote: I pleaded in terror.

name: Pip prays for mercy
source_quote: Pray don’t do it, sir.

name: Man speaks to Pip
source_quote: Once more,” said the man, staring at me.

name: Man demands action from Pip
source_quote: Give it mouth!

name: Man stares at Pip
source_quote: staring at me.

name: Man asks Pip to show where he lives
source_quote: Show us where you live,” said the man.

name: Man demands Pip to point out the place
source_quote: Pint out the place!

name: Pip points to the village
source_quote: I pointed to where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards, a mile or more from the church.
location: in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

name: Man looks at Pip
source_quote: The man, after looking at me for a moment...
location: in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

name: Man turns Pip upside down
source_quote: turned me upside down
location: in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

name: Man empties Pip's pockets
source_quote: emptied my pockets
location: in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

name: Pip finds nothing in pockets
source_quote: There was nothing in them but a piece of bread.
location: in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

name: Church comes to itself
source_quote: when the church came to itself

name: Pip sees the steeple under his feet
source_quote: I saw the steeple under my feet

name: Pip is seated on a high tombstone
source_quote: I was seated on a high tombstone

name: Pip trembles
source_quote: trembling while he ate the bread ravenously

name: Man eats the bread ravenously
source_quote: he ate the bread ravenously

name: Man comments on Pip's appearance
source_quote: You young dog,” said the man, licking his lips, “what fat cheeks you ha’ got.

name: Pip perceives the size of others
source_quote: I believe they were fat, though I was at that time undersized for my years, and not strong.

name: Pip reflects on his own size and strength
source_quote: I believe they were fat, though I was at that time undersized for my years, and not strong.

name: Man expresses desire to eat
source_quote: Darn me if I couldn’t eat ’em,” said the man, with a threatening shake of his head, “and if I han’t half a mind to’t!

name: Man shakes his head threateningly
source_quote: with a threatening shake of his head

name: Pip expresses hope
source_quote: I earnestly expressed my hope that he wouldn’t

name: Pip holds tighter to the tombstone
source_quote: held tighter to the tombstone on which he had put me

name: Pip holds tighter to the tombstone to avoid crying
source_quote: partly, to keep myself from crying

name: Man asks about Pip's mother
source_quote: “Now lookee here!” said the man. “Where’s your mother?”

name: Pip starts running
source_quote: He started, made a short run

name: Pip stops running
source_quote: and stopped

name: Pip looks over his shoulder
source_quote: and looked over his shoulder

name: Pip explains his relationship to Georgiana
source_quote: There, sir!” I timidly explained. “Also Georgiana. That’s my mother.

name: Pip expresses surprise
source_quote: Oh!

name: Pip returns to the conversation
source_quote: said he, coming back

name: Pip inquires about family
source_quote: And is that your father alonger your mother?

name: Pip acknowledges presence of another person
source_quote: Yes, sir,” said I; “him too; late of this parish.

name: Pip is questioned about his living situation
source_quote: “Who d’ye live with,—supposin’ you’re kindly let to live, which I han’t made up my mind about?”

name: Magwitch expresses uncertainty about Pip's fate
source_quote: which I han’t made up my mind about

name: Introduction of Mrs. Joe Gargery
source_quote: My sister, sir,—Mrs. Joe Gargery,—wife of Joe Gargery, the blacksmith, sir.

name: Magwitch speaks to Pip
source_quote: “Blacksmith, eh?” said he.

name: Magwitch looks at his leg
source_quote: And looked down at his leg.

name: Magwitch looks at Pip and his leg
source_quote: After darkly looking at his leg and me several times

name: Magwitch approaches Pip's tombstone
source_quote: he came closer to my tombstone

name: Magwitch takes Pip by both arms
source_quote: took me by both arms

name: Magwitch tilts Pip back
source_quote: tilted me back as far as he could hold me

name: Magwitch looks powerfully into Pip's eyes
source_quote: his eyes looked most powerfully down into mine

name: Pip looks helplessly up into Magwitch's eyes
source_quote: and mine looked most helplessly up into his

name: Magwitch questions Pip about living
source_quote: “Now lookee here,” he said, “the question being whether you’re to be let to live. You know what a file is?”

name: Pip feels helplessness and danger
source_quote: so as to give me a greater sense of helplessness and danger.

name: Pip is tilted over
source_quote: After each question he tilted me over a little more

name: Request for a file
source_quote: You get me a file.

name: Request for wittles
source_quote: And you get me wittles.

name: Demand to bring items
source_quote: You bring ’em both to me.

name: Threat of violence
source_quote: Or I’ll have your heart and liver out.

name: Repeated tilting
source_quote: He tilted me again.

name: Pip feels frightened
source_quote: I was dreadfully frightened

name: Pip feels giddy
source_quote: and so giddy

name: Pip clings to the man
source_quote: I clung to him with both hands

name: Pip requests assistance
source_quote: and said, 'If you would kindly please to let me keep upright, sir'

name: Pip expresses a desire to avoid sickness
source_quote: perhaps I shouldn’t be sick

name: Pip expresses a desire to attend more
source_quote: and perhaps I could attend more

name: Pip experiences a tremendous dip and roll
source_quote: He gave me a most tremendous dip and roll, so that the church jumped over its own weathercock.

name: Magwitch holds Pip by the arms
source_quote: Then, he held me by the arms, in an upright position on the top of the stone.

name: Magwitch speaks in fearful terms
source_quote: and went on in these fearful terms:—

name: Magwitch demands items from Pip
source_quote: You bring me, to-morrow morning early, that file and them wittles.

name: Magwitch threatens Pip
source_quote: You fail, or you go from my words in any partickler, no matter how small it is, and your heart and your liver shall be tore out, roasted, and ate.

name: Magwitch reveals he is not alone
source_quote: Now, I ain’t alone, as you may think I am.

name: Magwitch mentions the young man
source_quote: There’s a young man hid with me, in comparison with which young man I am a Angel.

name: Magwitch states the young man hears him
source_quote: That young man hears the words I speak.

name: Young man has a secret way of getting at a boy
source_quote: That young man has a secret way pecooliar to himself, of getting at a boy, and at his heart, and at his liver.

name: Boy attempts to hide from the young man
source_quote: It is in vain for a boy to attempt to hide himself from that young man.

name: Boy locks his door
source_quote: A boy may lock his door.

name: Boy is warm in bed
source_quote: may be warm in bed.

name: Boy tucks himself up
source_quote: may tuck himself up.

name: Boy draws the clothes over his head
source_quote: may draw the clothes over his head.

name: Boy thinks himself comfortable and safe
source_quote: may think himself comfortable and safe.

name: Young man softly creeps to the boy
source_quote: that young man will softly creep and creep his way to him.

name: Young man tears the boy open
source_quote: and tear him open.

name: Speaker is keeping the young man from harming the boy
source_quote: I am a keeping that young man from harming of you at the present moment, with great difficulty.

name: Speaker finds it hard to hold the young man off
source_quote: I find it wery hard to hold that young man off of your inside.

name: Speaker asks the boy what he says
source_quote: Now, what do you say?

name: Promise to retrieve the file
source_quote: I said that I would get him the file

name: Promise to bring broken bits of food
source_quote: I would get him what broken bits of food I could

name: Plan to visit at the Battery
source_quote: I would come to him at the Battery, early in the morning

name: Man threatens with curse
source_quote: “Say Lord strike you dead if you don’t!” said the man.

name: Pip reminds someone of their undertaking
source_quote: Now,” he pursued, “you remember what you’ve undertook, and you remember that young man, and you get home!

name: Pip instructs someone to go home
source_quote: Now,” he pursued, “you remember what you’ve undertook, and you remember that young man, and you get home!

name: He expresses a wish to be a frog or an eel
source_quote: I wish I was a frog. Or a eel!

name: He glances about him
source_quote: said he, glancing about him over the cold wet flat.

name: Pip observes the man limping
source_quote: As I saw him go, picking his way among the nettles, and among the brambles that bound the green mounds...

name: Pip feels fear about the man
source_quote: he looked in my young eyes as if he were eluding the hands of the dead people...

name: The man hugs himself
source_quote: he hugged his shuddering body in both his arms,—clasping himself, as if to hold himself together,—

name: The man limps towards the wall
source_quote: and limped towards the low church wall.

name: The man picks his way among the nettles and brambles
source_quote: picking his way among the nettles, and among the brambles that bound the green mounds...

name: Pip sees the man turning
source_quote: When I saw him turning, I set my face towards home...

name: Pip sets his face towards home
source_quote: I set my face towards home...

name: Pip makes the best use of his legs
source_quote: and made the best use of my legs.

name: Pip looks over his shoulder
source_quote: But presently I looked over my shoulder...

name: Pip sees the man going towards the river
source_quote: and saw him going on again towards the river...

name: The man hugs himself
source_quote: still hugging himself in both arms...

name: The man picks his way among the stones
source_quote: picking his way with his sore feet among the great stones...

name: Pip stops to look
source_quote: as I stopped to look after him

name: Pip perceives the marshes
source_quote: The marshes were just a long black horizontal line then

name: Pip perceives the river
source_quote: and the river was just another horizontal line

name: Pip perceives the sky
source_quote: and the sky was just a row of long angry red lines and dense black lines intermixed

name: Pip perceives the black things
source_quote: I could faintly make out the only two black things in all the prospect that seemed to be standing upright

name: Pip identifies the beacon
source_quote: one of these was the beacon by which the sailors steered

name: Pip describes the beacon
source_quote: an ugly thing when you were near it

name: Pip identifies the gibbet
source_quote: the other, a gibbet, with some chains hanging to it which had once held a pirate

name: A man limps towards the gibbet
source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter

name: Pip perceives the man as the pirate
source_quote: as if he were the pirate come to life

name: The man goes back to the gibbet
source_quote: and come down, and going back to hook himself up again

name: Pip feels a terrible turn
source_quote: It gave me a terrible turn when I thought so;

name: Pip perceives cattle lifting their heads
source_quote: as I saw the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him,

name: Pip wonders about the cattle's thoughts
source_quote: I wondered whether they thought so too.

name: Pip looks around for the young man
source_quote: I looked all round for the horrible young man,

name: Pip sees no signs of the young man
source_quote: and could see no signs of him.

name: Pip feels frightened again
source_quote: But now I was frightened again,

name: Pip runs home
source_quote: and ran home without stopping.
